rationale: The US-Iran deal remains the dominant story for Americans — Trump claims Sunday signing but Tehran disputes it and it's not on his public schedule; Israeli strikes on Beirut add complication. Tren de Aragua leader killing is a continuation of established pattern.

top_events.title: US-Iran deal: Trump claims Sunday signing, Tehran disputes, not on public schedule
top_events.summary: Contradictory signals on a landmark US-Iran deal create uncertainty for Americans — a signed deal could lower oil prices and reduce Middle East military risk, but no confirmation yet.

top_events.title: Israel attacks Beirut on day of expected Iran deal signing
top_events.summary: Israeli strikes on Beirut on the same day Trump claimed a deal would be signed complicate US diplomatic efforts and raise risk of broader Middle East entanglement for the US.

top_events.title: US kills leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua gang in air strike
top_events.summary: Continuation of Trump administration's airstrike campaign against criminal organizations — reinforces domestic security messaging but no new policy shift.
